name: India
headline: India BASELINE-MISMATCH RESOLUTION + CONDUCT CONTINUATION at 28.1 / Developing. Scanner flagged mismatch RS=32.4 vs INDEX=28.1 (4.3pt, RS_HIGH). Applied INDEX 28.1 per protocol — but noting that May 14-17 proposal arc (cumulative -7.8 from canonical 34.4) suggests RS reflects an earlier baseline. INDEX 28.1 is canonical and is the post-May-17 working baseline. New today: irregular Chenab River releases detected this week (Pakistan alleges intentional water weaponization at agricultural planting moment). Eurasia Group: India has 'effectively weaponized water' by suspending treaty obligations and withholding hydrological data. Sub-threshold negative; no new dimensional weight beyond May 17 ACC/INT downgrade. Confirm at 28.1; watch May 17 arbitration-rejection ACC dock for application.

baselineCorrection.note: Scanner-flagged CRITICAL mismatch RS=32.4 vs INDEX=28.1. INDEX is canonical truth. The May 14-17 proposal arc proposed -7.8 from 34.4 to 26.6 (final May 17 proposal). INDEX 28.1 may pre-date May 17 application; treat as canonical working baseline.

EMP.rationale: Sustained. Downstream Pakistani civilian impact (240M people, 80%+ agricultural water dependence) scored under Pakistan as harmed party.

BND.rationale: Sustained. IWT abeyance is underlying boundary act (April 23 2025). Chenab irregular releases are escalation but already absorbed into structural-conditions weight.

ACC.rationale: Sustained at canonical INDEX level. May 17 proposed -0.25 (arbitration-rejection ACC dock) is pending application; not double-counted today.

INT.rationale: Sustained at canonical INDEX level. May 17 proposed -0.25 (INT values-alignment dock) is pending application; not double-counted today.

evidence.finding: Pakistan accuses India of weaponizing water at critical agricultural planting moment.
Comprehensive analysis: how water is weaponized in India-Pakistan conflict. India lowered sluice gates at Baglihar and Salal dams, reducing Chenab flow into Pakistan by up to 90%.
Eurasia Group: India has 'effectively weaponized water' by suspending treaty obligations and withholding hydrological data. Shift toward upstream control as geopolitical leverage.
India formally rejected the May 15 PCA Supplemental Award upholding IWT validity; MEA spokesperson Jaiswal called it 'null and void.'
India-Pakistan unable to restore IWT; structural condition durable.

keyEvidence: Baseline correction: INDEX 28.1 applied (RS 32.4 discarded per protocol).
Pakistan alleges water weaponization at agricultural planting moment.
Chenab flow reduction by up to 90% via sluice gates at Baglihar/Salal dams.
Carry-forward: PCA Supplemental Award rejection (May 17) — deliberate-non-compliance-with-binding-international-arbitration ACC methodology category.
May 17 ACC -0.25, INT -0.25 proposed downgrade pending application.
rationale: Baseline-mismatch resolution + conduct continuation at 28.1. The Eurasia Group characterization ('effectively weaponized water') and Pakistan's specific Chenab-release allegations reinforce the deliberate-non-compliance-with-binding-international-arbitration ACC methodology category (formalized May 17) without adding new dimensional weight beyond the pending -1.5 downgrade. Hold at canonical INDEX 28.1 to avoid double-counting May 17 proposal. Math: (2.5+2.25+2.5+1.5+2.25+1.75+2.25+2.0)/8 = 17/8 = 2.125 → ((2.125-1)/4)*100 = 28.125 → 28.1.
whyHeadline: India baseline corrected (RS 32.4 to INDEX 28.1); Eurasia Group: India 'effectively weaponized water.'

dominantDimension.note: ACC carries the strongest negative signal (May 17 -0.25 pending). INDEX baseline held.

nextForwardSignal.label: India hydroelectric project escalation; Pakistan UNSC follow-up; PCA further ruling

watches: AFSPA response to UN experts letter
carryForward: Cumulative -7.8 composite delta from canonical published 34.4 (proposed)

math_check.validator_check: Math clean. Baseline-mismatch resolution at canonical INDEX value.
